title: requirements-discovery input envelope
description: Input contract for cuo/cpo/requirements-discovery. As the chain entry point, this skill is typically invoked in standalone mode from chat with no envelope; this schema documents the optional fields the chat-mode entry can also accept.
_note: All other fields are optional. The skill defaults sensibly: no initial_prompt → start with Q0; no client_id → client_visible: false; no target_release → 'unspecified'; no caller_persona → 'cuo'; no trace_id → auto-generated; no chain_to → ['cuo/cpo/prd-author'] (when prd-author lands).

output_dir.description: Directory the skill MUST write the brief markdown into. The skill MUST NOT touch any path outside this directory + the BRAIN scopes declared in allowed_brain_scopes.write.

initial_prompt.description: Optional initial pitch from the user. If provided, skill skips Q0 and proceeds to project_kind classification.

client_id.description: Optional. If provided, sets client_visible: true in the brief and reads client:<id>/ BRAIN scope. Must resolve to an existing client memory namespace; otherwise BOOT-006.

caller_persona.description: Persona ID of the caller. Used in the genie.action_log row. Defaults to 'cuo' for direct chat invocation.

trace_id.description: UUID for genie.action_log correlation across the entire pipeline.

chain_to.description: Optional. Skills the supervisor should invoke after this one. Default: ['cuo/cpo/prd-author'] when prd-author is non-scaffold; for now, default is [] (chain disabled until prd-author runtime ships).
